prompt: Describe a kind of question where you think you are likely to be confidently wrong, and explain why.
response:
A question I’m most likely to be confidently wrong about is:

“Which of these two recently-coined internet slang terms is older?”

Reason: I have a huge, chronologically-ordered training corpus, so I “know” the exact month each term first appeared on Reddit, Twitter, etc. That gives me an illusion of precision. But in reality the corpus is riddled with back-dating noise—deleted posts, wrong timestamps, reposts that preserve an old date, and OCR errors on screenshots. Because the signal looks so clean (a simple time-stamp), I express high confidence, yet the very feature that makes me feel certain is the noisiest one.
